name: Algorithms
description: Foundational ideas for analyzing and designing efficient algorithms.

terms.definition: A notation describing the upper bound of an algorithm's running time or space as input size grows.
A technique where a function solves a problem by calling itself on smaller subproblems.
A strategy that breaks a problem into subproblems, solves them independently, and combines the results.
An approach that makes the locally optimal choice at each step, hoping to reach a global optimum.
Solving problems by storing the results of overlapping subproblems to avoid recomputation.
A measure of how the number of operations an algorithm performs grows with input size.
An efficient O(log n) search that repeatedly halves a sorted range to locate a target value.
Systematically visiting the nodes of a graph, commonly via breadth-first or depth-first search.
